explanation: The user instructs to pick up the knife and then slice the tomato. The knife is initially on top of the fridge, so I first need to go to it. After picking up the knife, I need to go to the tomato, which is on the countertop. Since the knife is now being held, I can slice the tomato. The slicing action requires the object (tomato) to be placed on a receptacle (countertop_0), which is already in the state. Therefore, I place the tomato on the countertop before slicing it. The plan ensures proper sequence and adherence to physical constraints (holding one object at a time, requiring knife to be held for slicing).
